hawkish_phrases: inflation remained far too high and was projected to stay above the Governing Council's 2% medium-term target for too long
interest rates would still have to rise significantly at a steady pace to reach levels that were sufficiently restrictive
the risk of longer-term inflation expectations becoming unanchored remained elevated
core inflation was projected to remain persistently high, with underlying inflationary pressure strengthening further
A large number of members initially expressed a preference for increasing the key ECB interest rates by 75 basis points
dovish_phrases: The euro area economy was expected to contract in the current quarter and the first quarter of next year
a recession would be relatively short-lived and shallow
the Governing Council had to be careful not to dampen demand excessively as rates moved into restrictive territory
Some of these members, nonetheless, expressed their willingness to agree on a 50 basis point rate rise
uncertainties surrounding the state of the economy and the inflation outlook
summary: The Governing Council decided to raise key ECB interest rates by 50 basis points after a large number of members initially preferred 75 basis points, with communication emphasizing that rates would still need to rise significantly at a steady pace to sufficiently restrictive levels given inflation projections remaining above target through 2025.
